en: Construction work must be notified to the competent labor inspectorate prior to commencement where: (1) the work is expected to exceed five working days and involve more than 20 workers simultaneously, or (2) the total volume of work exceeds 500 person-days.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 3(1)
Workers may only be assigned tasks for which they are physically and mentally fit.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 5(4)

Where employees of multiple employers are working at the same construction site, employers must cooperate in the implementation of safety and health protection measures and coordinate their activities accordingly.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 3a(1)9

Multi-employer coordination must ensure protective measures do not create risks to others. Work coordination on construction sites to prevent safety and health risks must be ensured.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 4(6)\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 8(3), 13(3)
Qualified site supervision must be assigned for construction work.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 4(1)
Unauthorized access must be prevented using barriers or warning notices.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 4(7)
